markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' confirmation of a November 19, 2026 release date for Grand Theft Auto VI has intensified trader focus on which major events will precede the launch. Strong consensus around a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire reflects ongoing diplomatic momentum and its broad cultural ripple effects, while Drake's Iceman album sits close behind due to recent promotional singles and the artist's history of timely drops. Upcoming catalysts include Take-Two Interactive's May earnings call, potential GTA VI pre-order windows around May 18, and possible Trailer 3 timing, all of which could clarify marketing ramps and shift sentiment on music or pop-culture milestones before the console debut.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' official November 19, 2026 release window, announced after two successive delays from the original Fall 2025 and May 26, 2026 targets, has cemented near-unanimous trader conviction that GTA VI will miss June. Company statements emphasize the need for additional polish to meet long-standing quality expectations, while Take-Two Interactive's reaffirmed fiscal guidance leaves little room for an earlier launch. With the next earnings call scheduled for May 21 likely to provide only incremental updates on pre-orders and marketing rather than timeline shifts, the market reflects a stable consensus built on verified developer communications and historical patterns of extended Rockstar development cycles. A surprise early drop remains theoretically possible but would require an unprecedented reversal of public commitments.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial, declared after jurors deadlocked 9-3 for acquittal on May 15, has sharply tilted trader sentiment toward no additional prison time. The disgraced former Hollywood producer already sits behind bars on his separate California sexual assault conviction, and this latest hung jury—following a similar outcome in 2025—leaves prosecutors weighing whether to pursue a fourth trial on the single third-degree rape count that carries a maximum of four years. With Weinstein having served more time than that sentence would require, the market-implied 83.6 percent probability on “No Prison Time” reflects widespread doubt that Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg will retry the case, especially given the repeated credibility challenges against accuser Jessica Mann and the broader fatigue surrounding the long-running #MeToo saga. Upcoming developments, including a June 24 hearing on whether charges will be dropped, are now the key catalysts that could further lock in this outcome or introduce limited downside risk.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2025-26 NHL season has positioned the Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes as the clear frontrunners for the Stanley Cup, with their implied probabilities reflecting dominant regular-season records of 121 and 113 points, respectively, plus strong playoff positioning. The Avalanche's depth, led by consistent scoring and goaltending, has fueled a Presidents' Trophy-contending campaign and a dramatic second-round comeback victory over the Minnesota Wild. Meanwhile, the Hurricanes have maintained Eastern Conference supremacy through disciplined defensive play and home-ice dominance, recently completing a sweep of the Philadelphia Flyers. Vegas, Montreal, and Buffalo trail due to tougher paths in their respective conferences, though their playoff qualification and recent form keep modest upside in the tightly contested futures market.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2025-26 NHL season has positioned the Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes as the clear frontrunners for the Stanley Cup, with their implied probabilities reflecting dominant regular-season records of 121 and 113 points, respectively, plus strong playoff positioning. The Avalanche's depth, led by consistent scoring and goaltending, has fueled a Presidents' Trophy-contending campaign and a dramatic second-round comeback victory over the Minnesota Wild. Meanwhile, the Hurricanes have maintained Eastern Conference supremacy through disciplined defensive play and home-ice dominance, recently completing a sweep of the Philadelphia Flyers. Vegas, Montreal, and Buffalo trail due to tougher paths in their respective conferences, though their playoff qualification and recent form keep modest upside in the tightly contested futures market.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's 64-18 regular-season record and undefeated 8-0 playoff start, capped by a sweep of the Lakers, have solidified their status as the clear consensus favorite at 58.5% implied probability to repeat as NBA champions. Their depth, defensive versatility, and star core have fueled efficient postseason wins while limiting opponents' scoring. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 22.9% after a 62-20 campaign and advancement past Minnesota, anchored by Victor Wembanyama's two-way dominance and the team's elite home form. New York Knicks at 14.3% earned their spot by sweeping Philadelphia to reach the Eastern Conference Finals, relying on balanced scoring and defensive rebounding. Detroit Pistons and Cleveland Cavaliers trail at 3.9% and 1.9%, respectively, as their physical Eastern semifinal series remains undecided and trails the Western Conference's established frontrunners.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities among France, Spain, England, Brazil, and Argentina stems from deep roster talent, recent international form, and favorable group-stage positioning as the 48-team tournament nears. France has climbed to the top of FIFA rankings after convincing March results, while Spain carries momentum from its Euro 2024 title and youth infusion. England’s consistent qualifying record and Brazil-Argentina pedigree keep them within striking distance, with squad announcements and injury updates now directly shaping short-term odds shifts. No single side holds decisive edges in depth or matchups, sustaining a wide-open field until knockout rounds begin.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities among France, Spain, England, Brazil, and Argentina stems from deep roster talent, recent international form, and favorable group-stage positioning as the 48-team tournament nears. France has climbed to the top of FIFA rankings after convincing March results, while Spain carries momentum from its Euro 2024 title and youth infusion. England’s consistent qualifying record and Brazil-Argentina pedigree keep them within striking distance, with squad announcements and injury updates now directly shaping short-term odds shifts. No single side holds decisive edges in depth or matchups, sustaining a wide-open field until knockout rounds begin.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s sustained dominance within the Communist Party of China, reinforced by institutional reforms and ongoing anti-corruption purges in the military and elite ranks, underpins the 93 percent implied probability that he remains in power through the end of 2026. Recent actions, including his April 2025 symposium on the 15th Five-Year Plan and April 2026 directives on energy security amid global tensions, demonstrate active steering of long-term policy priorities without any signals of succession. Preparations for the 2027 Party Congress, where analysts widely anticipate a fourth term, further consolidate his position as general secretary and Central Military Commission chairman. Traders price these developments as evidence of entrenched authority, with no verifiable challenges or health-related disruptions emerging to shift the consensus in the near term.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom maintains the strongest position among potential 2028 Democratic presidential nominees, driven by repeated early polling leads and a May endorsement from filmmaker Steven Spielberg that signals initial major-donor momentum. His edge stems from executive experience in the nation's largest state and a prominent national profile opposing Republican priorities. Former Vice President Kamala Harris ranks next through broad name recognition yet faces donor caution after the prior cycle, while Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez attracts progressive enthusiasm in the fragmented field that also features Pete Buttigieg, Josh Shapiro, and Jon Ossoff. Differentiators center on governing records, ideological appeal, geographic bases, and fundraising reach. Consolidation will likely hinge on 2026 midterm outcomes, shifting primary surveys, and the party's coordinated response to federal Republican governance.
